sourceMovie.title: Revenge
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Revenge (2017) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Coralie Fargeat's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Hunt
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Hunt (2020) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Revenge rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Quick and the Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Quick and the Dead (1995) is the kind of film Revenge fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the bittersweet emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Grindhouse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Grindhouse shares Revenge's gift for action — the 2007 film delivers horror without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Becky
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Revenge compelling — the action, the horror — shows up in Becky (2020) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: You're Next
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of Revenge is alive in You're Next (2013), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Bone Tomahawk
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Bone Tomahawk (2015) operates in the same horror as Revenge, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Kraven the Hunter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Kraven the Hunter (2024) belongs to the same conversation as Revenge — both anchor their action in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: High Tension
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Revenge's horror stayed with you, High Tension (2003) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Silencing
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Revenge excels in action, The Silencing (2020) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Psycho II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Psycho II (1983) is the natural next step after Revenge: same horror DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

faq.question: What should I watch after Revenge?
faq.answer: Try The Hunt, The Quick and the Dead, Grindhouse—each shares DNA with Revenge while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Revenge?
faq.answer: Revenge (2017) is best described as Action, Horror, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Revenge worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
